Agentforce Revenue Management Learning Journey

﻿

Agentforce Revenue Management Fundamentals

Get an overview of Agentforce Revenue Management and see how it supports product catalog, pricing, contracts, and orders to streamline the entire revenue lifecycle.


Boost Sales Efficiency with Agentforce Revenue Management

Discover how Agentforce Revenue Management helps sales teams close deals faster through guided selling, and automation.


﻿Configure Agentforce Revenue Management

Learn to configure Agentforce Revenue Management components to deliver seamless end-to-end processes.
Name: Revenue Cloud Fundamentals

Revenue Cloud Certification


Get exam-ready for the Salesforce Revenue Cloud Consultant Certification. Review key concepts, explore scenarios, and strengthen your knowledge across CPQ, and Product-to-Cash lifecycle.


Prepare For Your Salesforce Revenue Cloud Consultant Exam

Product Catalog Management: Quick Look

Name: Product Catalog Management Learning Block 3

RLM_Learning_Description__c:
We've got Pricing set up for you. If you need to check the setup or review the steps, just refer to this.


With Salesforce Pricing, your users can create customized price adjustment methods that define how discounts are applied to your products and services. Pricing procedures help you create precise formulas for calculating the final net price, and provide complete visibility into the pricing calculation process.


Pricing Recipe

  • Every pricing org requires a pricing recipe. When Salesforce Pricing is activated, your org is automatically set up with a predefined recipe.

Select a Pricing Procedure


  • To select a pricing procedure, you must first clone the predefined expression set templates available. You can also build a custom pricing procedure to meet the unique needs of your business.

Record Sharing


  • For runtime users to access the data created by product designers or catalog admins, set up record sharing for all the Pricing entities.

Set Up Price Logs


  • To collect logs captured by pricing APIs, turn on Price Logs Capture.

Set Up Objects for Salesforce Pricing


  • There are recommended Pricing objects configurations described. You can set up Pricing objects based on your requirements.


↗ Show Me How to Set Up Pricing in Salesforce Help
Name: Price Management Validate Popup Block

RLM_Learning_Description__c: To get hands-on with Salesforce Pricing capabilities, Launch the App.
Name: Salesforce Pricing Launch App Popup Block

RLM_Learning_Description__c:
With the comprehensive solutions of Agentforce Revenue Management, your sales reps can use Transaction Management to:


  • Access product catalogs with Product Catalog Management.
  • Configure products to meet your business requirements with Product Configurator.
  • Price products with Salesforce Pricing.
  • Define rates for your customers based on their consumption of your products with Rate Management.
  • Design and track intricate order fulfillment processes with Dynamic Revenue Orchestrator.
  • Configure tax calculations for your transactions by using an external tax engine provider.


Transaction Management Essentials: Before you configure Transaction Management, learn about its features, considerations, limits, available personas, and permissions.


Customize Transaction Management: To provide the necessary information and a personalized experience for your sales reps, sales operations reps, and partners, customize page layouts and Transaction Management components.


Sales Transactions in Revenue Cloud: Manage different types of sales transactions such as quotes, orders, contracts, and assets seamlessly in Agentforce Revenue Management.


↗ Explore Transaction Management in Salesforce Help
Name: Transaction Management Get Started Popup Text Block

RLM_Learning_Description__c:
Product Catalog Management includes various permission sets for users. These users or personas perform various roles in implementing and managing your product portfolio. These manual steps represent one option for creating data in the org. Customers can also consider alternative options for importing data, either from files or from other orgs. The data can include products, pricing, and related rules.


  • Object Setup: To complete the setup of Product Catalog Management, adjust the page layout and field access on the product catalog objects.
  • Record Sharing: For runtime users to access the data created by product designers or catalog admins, set up record sharing for all PCM entities.

↗ Show Me How to Set Up Transaction Management in Salesforce Help
Name: Transaction Management Validate Setup Popup Text Block

RLM_Learning_Description__c: To get hands-on with Transaction Management, Launch the App.
Name: Transaction Management Launch App Popup Text Block

RLM_Learning_Description__c:
Automate and optimize the quote creation or order capture experience with the right products and right prices whether its an initial, amendment, or renewal sale.


  • Quote and Order Capture: Configure, price, and sell products with Quote and Order Capture in Revenue Lifecycle Management. Quote and Order Capture supports subscription lifecycles and ensures end-to-end integrity of your quotes and orders.
  • Asset Lifecycle: Get insights into customer assets and increase customer lifetime value by using Revenue Lifecycle Management. Also, get a consolidated list of all assets that belong to an account.

↗ Go to the Transaction Management Developer Guide
Name: Transaction Management Customize Capabilities Popup Text Block

RLM_Learning_Description__c:
You can use Dynamic Revenue Orchestrator to design and track the entire fulfilment process.


Get Started with Dynamic Revenue Orchestrator: See a high-level overview of the steps for setting up Dynamic Revenue Orchestrator (DRO).


Design-Time Administration: Design time is the time before a user submits an order, when a fulfillment designer is setting everything up. Set up the decomposition and orchestration during design time so that it's ready to go when an order is submitted.


Run-Time Administration: Run Time refers to the time after an order is submitted and fulfillment is happening. During run time, fulfillment operators track the decomposition and orchestration of products.


Dynamic Revenue Orchestrator Concepts and References: This section contains topics that explain the concepts and details that you must know to get the full power of DRO.


↗ Explore Dynamic Revenue Orchestrator in Salesforce Help
Name: DRO Get Started Popup Text Block

RLM_Learning_Description__c:
We've got Dynamic Revenue Orchestrator set up for you. If you need to check the setup or review the steps, just refer to this. Before you set up Dynamic Revenue Orchestrator, complete these prerequisites:


  • Set Up Product Catalog Management.
  • Set Up Salesforce Pricing.
  • Enable Revenue Settings (Set Up Orders).
  • Enable Dynamic Revenue Orchestrator: You must turn on Dynamic Revenue Orchestrator so that your org is prepared to use it for fulfillment.
  • Enable In-flight Amendments: Allow changes to fulfillment requests that are in progress.
  • Enable Future Dated Steps: Execute fulfillment steps on a future date.
  • Automate Order Submission to Dynamic Revenue Orchestrator: Create a record-triggered flow that automatically submits Agentforce Revenue Management orders to Dynamic Revenue Orchestrator when the orders are activated.
  • Set Up Fallout and Service Level Agreements Settings: Turn on features to manage failed or late fulfillment steps. Dynamic Revenue Orchestrator sets up certain decision tables, which are turned on automatically.
  • Create Queues: Create Salesforce queues to contain fatally failed steps: For example, you can create a queue for each group in your organization that handles fallout. These queues must include Fulfillment Step as a supported object.

↗ Show Me How to Set Up Dynamic Revenue Orchestrator in Salesforce Help
Name: DRO Validate Setup Popup Text Block

RLM_Learning_Description__c: Visit the Dynamic Revenue Orchestrator home page to manage fulfillment design tasks and processes. To get hands-on with Dynamic Revenue Orchestrator, Launch the App.
Name: DRO Launch App Popup Text

RLM_Learning_Description__c:
Get visibility into a product’s fulfillment journey. Also, get a complete view of the entire fulfillment design that includes processes such as order decomposition, fulfillment plans, and jeopardy management.


  • Dynamic Revenue Orchestrator Standard Objects: The Dynamic Revenue Orchestrator data model provides objects and fields to manage details of a product’s fulfillment.
  • Dynamic Revenue Orchestrator Standard Invocable Actions: Use standard invocable action to submit an order to Dynamic Revenue Orchestrator (DRO) for fulfillment.
  • Dynamic Revenue Orchestrator Metadata API Types: Metadata API enables you to access some types and feature settings that you can customize in the user interface.
  • Callouts in Dynamic Revenue Orchestrator: Use callout step types to make ‌HTTP calls to an external system. Callout step types use the Data Consumption Framework and Process Type Integration for interface management and communication with external systems.

↗ Go to the Dynamic Revenue Orchestrator Developer Guide
Name: DRO Customize Capabilities Popup Text

RLM_Learning_Description__c:
Monetize your sales models with Invoice Management. Bill in advance or arrears for recurring products and services. Automate and scale invoice generation. Configure multiple legal entities and tax engines. Convert negative invoice lines to credit memos automatically. Enhance financial reporting with accurate record-keeping using accounting periods.


Set Up and Configure Billing: Enable Billing, assign the required permission sets, and set up features.


Criteria for Invoicing Products: Define billing policies, treatments, and treatment items to generate invoices that suit your sales models. Specify billing criteria to define whether you want to bill your products in advance or in arrears, whether specific products are billed, and other conditions.


Tax Calculation for Invoices: Automatically calculate taxes on the billing amounts of your taxable products or services by using an external tax engine. Configure tax settings to authenticate the external tax engine and define rules for taxation.


Payment Terms for Invoices: Negotiate and track payment terms for invoices to set a due date for payments and collect payments in a timely manner. With payment terms, you can anticipate or enforce the date by when payments for outstanding invoices must be paid.


Financial Accounting in Revenue Cloud: Accounting periods store information about the time frames for which your company prepares business reports and monitors company performance. Use accounting periods for legal entities to relate their billing transactions with the accounting period in which they occur.


Dashboard Setup: Monitor invoices, credit memos, and invoice schedules from the Billing Operations Console. View key financial metrics in summary cards and manage invoice batch runs with timely insights into revenue transaction log errors and failed invoices.


Enable Timeline for Collections: Configure interactive timelines so that collections specialists and collections managers can view the key events related to collections on the timeline.


Set Up Salesforce Payment for Revenue Cloud: Set up an Experience Cloud site that's used as a data channel between the payment provider and the Salesforce Payments feature. Then, enable Salesforce Payments to collect payments for your transactions.


↗ Explore Billing Management in Salesforce Help
Name: Billing Get Started Popup Text

RLM_Learning_Description__c:
We've got Billing set up for you. If you need to check the setup or review the steps, just refer to this.


Enable Billing, assign the required permission sets, and set up features.


  • Enable Billing: Turn on Billing to start using Billing features.
  • Guided Set Up for Billing: The guided experience is structured to help Billing admins complete each step sequentially, ensuring your Billing setup is accurate and fully operational.
  • Set Up Billing Defaults: With Billing already enabled, the user will now have access to select system defaults for billing.
  • Payment Terms : Payment terms are used to establish guidelines on the timing and conditions for payments and invoice settlement between the buyer and seller. They are usually negotiated at the time of contract negotiation or creation, or order generation, and are then passed downstream to Billing.
  • Create Billing Schedules and Billing Schedule Groups: To bill orders, billing schedules and billing schedule groups must be created when orders are activated. For that to happen, you must clone a flow template called Order To Billing Schedule and activate the copy.

↗ Show Me How to Set Up Billing in Salesforce Help
Name: Billing Validate Setup Popup Block

RLM_Learning_Description__c: To get hands-on with Billing Management, Launch the App.
Name: Billing Launch App Popup Text

RLM_Learning_Description__c:
Get an integrated and extensible subscription and usage-based billing solution. Automate processes such as payment processing, invoice generation, and usage-based billing.


  • Billing Standard Objects: The Billing data model provides objects and fields to manage billing and tax configurations, credit memos, and invoices, and accounting periods for legal entities.
  • Billing Platform Events: Salesforce publishes standard platform events in response to an action that occurred in the org or to report errors. For example, the InvoiceProcessedEvent platform event sends notification to the customer when the billing invoice activity is complete. You can subscribe to a standard platform event by using the subscription mechanism that the event supports.
  • Billing Standard Invocable Actions: Learn more about the standard invocable actions available with Billing.
  • Billing Business APIs: Use the Billing Business APIs to manage credit application and to handle billing scenarios.
  • Billing Apex Reference: Billing provides the ConnectApi namespace (also called Connect in Apex) that contains classes for accessing the same capabilities that are available in the Billing Business APIs.
  • Billing Metadata API Types: Metadata API enables you to access some types and feature settings that you can customize in the user interface.

↗ Go to the Billing Developer Guide
Name: Billing Customize Capabilities Popup Block

RLM_Learning_Description__c:
Use Rate Management to define rates for usage resources tied to sellable products. Set rates based on consumption and adjust them according to quantity and attributes. Create precise formulas with rating procedures and use the rating waterfall view to understand rate breakdowns.


Discover and Explore: Learn about the licenses required to use rate management and the personas each license supports. Understand the key terms and things to consider when you implement rate management for your organization.


Rate Management Setup: To access rate management objects and define rate and rate adjustments, enable the rate management setting. To gain insights into the rate calculations, enable rating waterfall.


Rating Procedures: Rating procedures are customizable, ordered stacks of rating elements that are used to calculate the final net rate of a usage resource. Each rating element forms a step in a rating procedure. Rating elements call appropriate lookup tables to perform the rating calculations.


↗ Explore Rate Management in Salesforce Help
Name: Rate Management Get Started Popup Text Block

RLM_Learning_Description__c:
We've got Rate Management set up for you. If you need to check the setup or review the steps, just refer to this.


To access Rate Management objects and define rate and rate adjustments, enable the rate management setting. To gain insights into the rate calculations, enable rating waterfall.


  • Enable Rate Management: Give your users access to the Rate Management objects and features.
  • Enable Rating Waterfall: Rating Waterfall provides insights and reasons for every step of the rating process. Let's say that while calculating the final net rate of a usage resource, you find out that the additional taxes applied or the discount is less than anticipated. With Waterfall View, you can view the rate breakups at every step along with the reason for the addition or deduction.
  • Enable Rating Waterfall Persistence: Store the process logs that provide insights into the internal rating processes.
  • Create a Usage Resource: Usage resource is an entitlement or service that’s granted with the product sold to your customer. Before you start defining rates and rate adjustments for usage resources, you must create usage resources for your products.
  • Rate Cards and Rate Card Entries: Define the rules that are used to rate the consumption of a group of usage resources within a product. If the customer consumes the usage resource beyond the granted limits or period, specific rates are applied.

↗ Go to Rate Management Setup in Salesforce Help
Name: Rate Management Validate Setup Popup Block

RLM_Learning_Description__c: To get hands-on with Rate Management, Launch the App.
Name: Rate Management Launch App Popup Text Block

RLM_Learning_Description__c:
Quote and price products based on predefined rates for future use of the product or service.


  • Rate Management Standard Objects: The Rate Management data model provides objects and fields to manage rates and discounts for a product's resource consumption.
  • Rate Management Metadata API Types: Metadata API enables you to access some types and feature settings that you can customize in the user interface.
  • Rate Management Business APIs: Use the Rate Management Business APIs to get rate plan details.
  • Rate Management Standard Invocable Actions: Learn more about the standard invocable actions available with Rate Management.

↗ Go to the Rate Management Developer Guide
Name: Rate Management Customize Capabilities Popup Text Block

RLM_Learning_Description__c:
Salesforce Contracts automates and streamlines each stage of the B2B contract lifecycle:


  1. Authoring: Automatically generate contracts using customizable templates, auto-fill data from CRM, and ensure compliance with pre-approved terms.
  2. Negotiation: Track document versions, enable real-time collaboration, and automate approval workflows to ensure all parties agree on terms and conditions.
  3. Execution: Integrate with e-signature solutions for secure and fast electronic signing, while maintaining a complete audit trail.
  4. Post-Signature: Store contracts centrally, set renewal reminders, and track performance for effective contract management and compliance.

Salesforce Contracts reduces manual work, ensures consistency, and accelerates the contracting process from start to finish.


↗ Explore Contracts in Salesforce Help
Name: Salesforce Contracts Get Started Popup Text Block

RLM_Learning_Description__c:
After you enable the ContractManagement license in your org, you must complete the org setup and other admin tasks for the Salesforce Contracts functionalities to be available.


  1. Enable Salesforce Contracts :Grant users access to create and manage contracts by using Salesforce Contracts.
  2. Add Salesforce Contracts Licenses and Install Omnistudio Package: Before you can install an Omnistudio package into an org that doesn't already have an Omnistudio package, you must prepare your browser and your Salesforce org. Omnistudio is in a managed package that you install into a Salesforce org.
  3. Map Fields for Opportunity, Order, and Quote: Use the field mapping XML you can map fields between a contract and standard objects, standard BPOs, and custom objects.

↗ Show Me How to Set Up Contracts in Salesforce Help
Name: Salesforce Contracts Validate Setup Popup Text Block

RLM_Learning_Description__c: To discover Contract Lifecycle Management capabilities Launch App here.
Name: Salesforce Contracts Launch App Popup Text

RLM_Learning_Description__c:
Salesforce Contracts provides a highly usable, configurable, and extensible contracting platform that can be integrated with industry specific use cases. Salesforce Contracts functionality is centered around the "Contract" object of Salesforce, which is enriched and extended to support this new application.


  • Salesforce Contracts Standard Objects: Use standard objects to work with the Salesforce Contracts application.
  • Salesforce Contracts Resources: Salesforce Contracts Business APIs (CLM and E-signature) are RESTful APIs that are sometimes available as Apex classes and methods.
  • Invocable Actions for Salesforce Contracts: With invocable actions, create your own Salesforce Flows, triggering various functions within Salesforce Contracts. You can create document versions, create and update contracts, perform various contract actions, and send contracts for e-signature.

↗ Go to the Salesforce Contracts Developer Guide
Name: Salesforce Contracts Customize Capabilities Popup Text Block

RLM_Learning_Description__c:
You can access the Usage Management app from the app launcher. The home page of the Usage Management app is divided into sections such as Policy Definition and Consumption Details. These sections are available out-of-the-box in Usage Management and are customizable.


  • Discover and Explore Usage Management : Understand required licenses and supported personas for Usage Management, along with implementation considerations for your organization.
  • Usage Management Setup: To access Usage Management objects, enable the Usage Management setting. Set up Usage Modeling to manage consumption of usage-based products.
  • Rate Management Setup: Define rates and adjustments for usage resources, allowing the computing engine to calculate customer charges based on consumption.
  • Usage Selling: Usage Selling allows selling products with usage benefits, creating quotes, orders, and records for consumption management and resource wallets.
  • Consumption Management: Track consumption, calculate overages, and generate invoice-ready summaries, including consumed units, overages, rates, and total payable amounts.
  • Wallet Management: The usage management process automatically creates wallets for each purchased usage resource for each account.

↗ Read More
Name: Usage Management Get Started Popup Block

RLM_Learning_Description__c:
We've got Usage Management set up for you. If you need to check the setup or review the steps, just refer to this.


Usage Management includes various permission sets for users. These steps represent one option for creating data in the org. The various setups needed are:


  • Create Products for Usage : A product is considered sellable when it provides grants. You can create a sellable product of type Anchor or Pack. An anchor product is the main product or service, and a pack is an add-on product or service that provides additional units of usage resources.
  • Create Unit of Measure: A unit of measure quantifies the consumption of a usage resource. You can define units of measure to support a wide range of usage resources. For example, a unit of measure can be time-based, volume-based, transaction-based, or count-based.
  • Create Units of Measure Class: Unit of measure class groups the same type of units of measure. For example, the Currency unit of measure class groups various currencies such as Euro, Dollar, and Rupee.
  • Create Usage Aggregation Policies: Define the method used to aggregate consumption of a usage resource over a period.

↗ Show Me how to Set Up Usage Management in Salesforce Help
Name: Usage Management Validate Setup Popup Text Block

RLM_Learning_Description__c: To get hands-on with Usage Management capabilities, Launch the App.
Name: Usage Management Launch App Popup Text Block

Provide transparent, accurate, and efficient management of usage data and estimated usage amount to enhance revenue management capabilities.


  • Usage Management Standard Objects : The Usage Management data model provides objects and fields to setup and manage consumption of usage-based products.
  • Usage Management Standard Invocable Actions: Learn more about the standard invocable actions available with Usage Management.
  • Usage Management Metadata API Types: Metadata API enables you to access some types and feature settings that you can customize in the user interface.

RLM_Learning_Description__c:
Product Catalog Management allows administrators and designers to quickly create and manage product portfolios using attributes, classifications, simple/bundled products, and rules, enabling faster, more efficient catalog management at market-leading speed.


Glossary


  • Bundled Products: Bundled products are a group of products that are sold together as one unit. You can create static or configurable bundled products. For static products, you can’t add or remove child products, alter product quantities, or configure product attributes at run time.
  • Catalog: A catalog is a collection of the products that you sell, organized into categories for easier discoverability. You must create a catalog before you create catalog categories and assign products to the categories.

Learn More
Name: Product Catalog Management Top Block

RLM_Learning_Description__c:
You can access the Product Catalog Management app from the app launcher. The home page of the Product Catalog Management app is divided into sections such as Catalogs, Products, Attributes, and Rules. These sections are available out-of-the-box in Product Catalog Management and are customizable.


Catalogs: A catalog is a collection of the products that you sell, organized into categories for easier discoverability. With Product Catalog Management you can create two types of catalogs — Sales and Service Process.


Dynamic Attributes: Attributes are the characteristics or properties of products. For example, a mobile phone product can have attributes like size, weight, storage capacity, display size, and camera specifications.


Product Classification: A product classification is a template that you can use to quickly define and create products. Product classifications can be reused to create multiple products that are similar yet different.


Manage your products and product bundles, build your product catalogs, and more from the Product Catalog Management app's home page. Your admin can even customize it with the Lightning App Builder to perfectly fit your needs!
Name: PCM_Learn

RLM_Learning_Description__c:
Salesforce Pricing Learning Hub


Salesforce Pricing: Quick Look

Define a comprehensive pricing strategy to win deals and increase revenue.


Price Management with Agentforce Revenue Management

Use Salesforce Pricing to create custom pricing for your products.


Advanced Price Management with Agentforce Revenue Management

Expand your pricing skills with derived, contracts, and subscription-based pricing.
Name: Price Management Learning Block 2

RLM_Learning_Description__c:
Summer '26 Release Notes


  • CSV Based Decision Tables (Generally Available): Source decision tables from CSV uploads that support more than 30 inputs and 5 outputs — create a decision table with Type CSV, upload the file, activate it, and map it to a pricing element in your pricing procedure.
  • Pricing General Enhancements (Generally Available): The Revenue Operations Console adds a date and time hover and a currency column, and the Map Line Items element now supports up to 100 default tags and runs within Parallel Execution.

Read More
Name: Price Management Learning Block 1

RLM_Learning_Description__c:
Salesforce Pricing is a comprehensive, reliable, and user-friendly pricing solution. Your users can create customized price adjustment methods that define how discounts are applied to your products and services.


Glossary


  • Pricing Procedure : A Pricing Procedure is the price orchestration or pricing policy that users can define how to price their products or services. Users can also define custom logic and solutions to implement pricing strategies in any org.
  • Price Waterfall : Price Waterfall provides detailed information about the outcome of pricing execution for any transaction. It outlines the step-by-step process of how the products or services are priced and details any discounts or adjustments that the transaction underwent.

Learn More
Name: Price Management Solution Overview Block

RLM_Learning_Description__c:
Create customized price adjustment methods and pricing procedures. Determine the discounts to apply to your products and services by using price adjustment methods. Get visibility into the pricing calculation process by using pricing procedures.


Salesforce Pricing Standard Objects

  • The Salesforce Pricing data model provides objects and fields to manage pricing processes, such as product management and calculation and application of discounts.

Salesforce Pricing Business APIs

  • Perform pricing request, create context instance, sync pricing data, and manage pricing recipes and pricing waterfall details by using the Salesforce Pricing Business APIs.

Salesforce Pricing Standard Invocable Actions

  • Learn more about the standard invocable actions available with Salesforce Pricing.

Salesforce Pricing Metadata API Types

  • Metadata API enables you to access some types and feature settings that you can customize in the user interface.

Salesforce Pricing Tooling API Objects

  • Tooling API exposes metadata used in developer tooling that you can access through REST or SOAP. Tooling API’s SOQL capabilities for many metadata types allow you to retrieve smaller pieces of metadata.

↗ Go to the Salesforce Pricing Developer Guide
Name: Price Management Customize Capabilities Popup Text Block

RLM_Learning_Description__c:
Transaction Management Learning Hub


Efficient Sales with Agentforce Revenue Management

Learn how Transaction Management simplifies the quote-creation and order-capture experience.


Asset Lifecycle Management with Agentforce Revenue Management

Support customers and manage assets to strengthen post-sales relationship.
Name: Transaction Management Learning Block 1

RLM_Learning_Description__c:
Summer '26 Release Notes


  • Guided Ramp Schedule Generation with Trial and Prorated Segments (Generally Available): Replace slow manual cloning for multi-year ramp deals with guided generation that supports free or trial segments and prorated stub periods; reps start from the Create Ramp Schedule action.
  • Early Renewal for Ramped Asset (Generally Available): Renew a ramped asset ahead of schedule by setting a future renewal start date — the renewal quote replaces the remainder of the existing ramp schedule with new ramp segments.

Read More
Name: Transaction Management Learning Block 2

RLM_Learning_Description__c:
Transaction Management empowers sales reps to create and manage quotes, orders, renewals, and amendments, streamlining subscription lifecycles and ensuring transaction integrity. Reps can also create assets for insights into customer purchases, enhancing support and fostering long-term relationships.


Glossary


  • Asset: An asset is a product or service that your customers have purchased. In Agentforce Revenue Management, an asset has a lifecycle, where the asset can be amended, renewed, and cancelled.
  • Asset Action: Represents a change made to the Asset. Represents a change made to the Asset. Asset actions include initial sale, amendment, renewal, and cancellation.

Learn More
Name: Transaction Management Solution Overview Block

RLM_Learning_Description__c:
Dynamic Revenue Orchestrator Learning Hub


Dynamic Revenue Orchestrator Foundations

Handle orders with accuracy and efficiency to transform your order-to-cash process.
Name: DRO Learning Block 1

RLM_Learning_Description__c:
Summer '26 Release Notes


  • DRO Templates (Beta): Stand up orchestration faster with pre-built Dynamic Revenue Orchestrator templates.
  • DRO and OMS Interop (Beta): Keep Dynamic Revenue Orchestrator and your Order Management System in real-time sync, closing the gap where teams previously managed two non-synced platforms.

Read More
Name: DRO Learning Block 2

RLM_Learning_Description__c:
Dynamic Revenue Orchestrator (DRO) empowers businesses to streamline complex order fulfillment. Break down orders, apply custom fulfillment plans to each product, and deliver tailored customer experiences.


Glossary


  • Fulfillment Order: A fulfillment order groups fulfillment order line items. It can represent a technical order that’s placed on a fulfillment subsystem.
  • Jeopardy Management: The configuration of the estimated duration and the jeopardy threshold for a fulfillment step that’s monitored during fulfillment plan execution. This process raises an alert when the step isn’t completed within the configured SLA settings.

Learn More
Name: DRO Solution Overview Block

RLM_Learning_Description__c:
Monetize all sales models with Invoice Management. Bill in advance or arrears, automate invoice generation, and scale efficiently. Support multiple legal entities and tax engines. Automatically convert negative invoice lines to credit memos and apply credit to invoices. Ensure accurate record-keeping and improve financial reporting using accounting periods for legal entities.


Glossary


  • Billing Day of Month: Indicates the day of the month when customers are billed. This is applicable for products that have recurring billing.
  • Billing Frequency: The frequency at which customers are billed. Currently, it's derived from pricing term or pricing frequency.

Learn More
Name: Billing Solution Overview Block

RLM_Learning_Description__c:
Billing Management Learning Hub


Billing Basics in Agentforce Revenue Management

Learn Billing to automate invoicing, manage payments and credits, and gain visibility into full billing lifecycle.
Name: Billing Learning Hub Block 1

RLM_Learning_Description__c:
Summer '26 Release Notes


  • Statement of Account (New): Generate a consolidated view that summarizes all billing transactions — invoices, payments, credits, debits, and refunds — for an account over a period, on demand from the Account page (requires Document Generation enabled in Billing Settings).
  • Advanced Amendments (Enhanced): Generate accurate billing schedules for amendments tied to milestone billing, asset transfers, and coterminous contracts.

Read More
Name: Billing Learning Hub Block 3

RLM_Learning_Description__c: Use Rate Management to easily define the rates for usage resources granted with the sellable product. Set up rates based on consumption of a usage resource. Define rate adjustments based on the consumption quantity and rate-impacting attributes of a usage resource. Use rating procedures to create precise formulas for calculating the final net rate and use the rating waterfall view to understand the rate breakup steps of the rating calculation process.
Name: Rate Management Solution Overview

RLM_Learning_Description__c: Rate Management Learning Hub
Name: Rate Management Learning Block 1

RLM_Learning_Description__c:
Spring ’25 Release Notes


  • Optimize Usage Costs with Predefined Negotiable Rating Elements: Use the predefined negotiable rating procedure or create a rating procedure by using the negotiable rating elements to calculate negotiated rates. Use Rating Procedure Builder to calculate negotiated rates and make sure that you apply the most accurate and favorable rates for your usage resources, reflecting any special agreements made during the negotiation. Provide precise billing and maintain customer satisfaction by honoring the negotiated terms. Default to base rates when specific negotiations don't exist.
  • Manage the Lifecycle of Rate Card Entries Effectively: Simplify the transition of rate card entries from the draft to the active or the inactive state. Leverage the flexibility of status-based management to edit rate card entries in the draft status.

Read More
Name: Rate Management Learning Block 2

RLM_Learning_Description__c:
Salesforce Contracts Learning Hub


Transform Your Business with Salesforce Contracts

Digitize and modernize contract lifecycle management.
Name: Salesforce Contracts Learning Hub Block 1

RLM_Learning_Description__c:
Summer '26 Features


  • AI-Supported Bulk Contract Extraction (New): Extract metadata at scale from existing contract PDFs to unlock historical data trapped in legacy documents, protecting margins and increasing renewal revenue.
  • Advanced Approvals for Contracts (New): Run multi-stakeholder, serial-approval workflows on Contracts using the Approvals framework.

Read More
Name: Salesforce Contracts Learning Hub Block 2

RLM_Learning_Description__c:
Salesforce Contracts app enables full lifecycle management of contracts, from authoring to activation, integrating with opportunities, orders, quotes, and custom objects for streamlined sales and contract processes.


Leverage the power of Contracts AI


Contracts AI streamlines the labour-intensive process of drafting clauses for legal agreements and enables the efficient extraction of contract details and clauses from complex PDFs. Einstein boosts your productivity, accuracy, and compliance in the digital age.


Learn More
Name: Salesforce Contracts Solution Overview Block

RLM_Learning_Description__c:
Usage Management Learning Hub

Model, track, and manage consumption-based products from setup to invoice-ready summaries.


Go to Help Documents
Name: Usage Management Learning Block 1

RLM_Learning_Description__c:
Summer '26 Release Notes


  • Usage Product Guided Setup (Enhanced): A streamlined guided setup flow for configuring usage products.
  • Consumption Agent (Enhanced): Agentforce-powered assistance for usage selling and consumption insights (requires Revenue Cloud Billing and Agentforce).

Read More
Name: Usage Management Learning Block 2

RLM_Learning_Description__c:
Manage consumption of usage-based products with Usage Management. It includes usage modeling to simplify the setup of product definitions, rate management to establish and adjust rates, and consumption management to track consumption data. It also provides invoice-ready consumption summaries and wallet management to manage entitlements and facilitate drawdowns based on consumption data.


Glossary


  • Entitlements: Grants that are assigned with the sellable product that a customer purchases.
  • Liable Summary: Stores the calculated overages for the usage resource and the amount that’s charged for these overages.
  • Overages: Number of units that are consumed over the base limit of grants provided with the sellable product.

Learn More
Name: Usage Management Solution Overview Block

RLM_Learning_Description__c:
We've got Pricing set up for you. If you need to check the setup or review the steps, just refer to this.


With Salesforce Pricing, your users can create customized price adjustment methods that define how discounts are applied to your products and services. Pricing procedures help you create precise formulas for calculating the final net price, and provide complete visibility into the pricing calculation process.


Pricing Recipe

  • Every pricing org requires a pricing recipe. When Salesforce Pricing is activated, your org is automatically set up with a predefined recipe.

Select a Pricing Procedure


  • To select a pricing procedure, you must first clone the predefined expression set templates available. You can also build a custom pricing procedure to meet the unique needs of your business.

Record Sharing


  • For runtime users to access the data created by product designers or catalog admins, set up record sharing for all the Pricing entities.

Set Up Price Logs


  • To collect logs captured by pricing APIs, turn on Price Logs Capture.

Set Up Objects for Salesforce Pricing


  • There are recommended Pricing objects configurations described. You can set up Pricing objects based on your requirements.


↗ Show Me How to Set Up Pricing in Salesforce Help

  • Configuration Flow Template: An out-of-the-box template built on a flow that serves as the foundation for the run-time configurator.

RLM_Learning_Description__c:
The Product Configurator enables sales reps to instantly validate product selections and access real-time pricing insights. It streamlines complex product offerings, saving time, improving accuracy, and enhancing the customer experience. Key features include:


Learn and Explore Product Configurator: Learn how your users can use the product configurator. Understand its core features to provide a smooth and user-friendly buying experience for your customers.


Enable Product Configurator: Grant users access to the Product Configurator features.


Explore the Product Configurator Flow: The product configurator flow outlines the layout for presenting customizable product information in the user interface.


Work With the Product Configurator: Create complex product configurations with ease by using Product Configurator. Define attributes, variants, and metadata.


Configuration Rules: When you configure products for sale, ensure that all your products have valid attributes and are compatible with other products in the offering.


Third-Party Configurator: Agentforce Revenue Management supports various third-party use cases such as- party configurator with the third-party configurator, integrating third-party party UI components into the first-party configurator, and creating custom configurator UI using headless configurator APIs.


This tool is ideal for businesses offering complex products, enhancing both the sales process and customer satisfaction.


↗ Explore Product Configurator in Salesforce Help
Name: ProductConfig_Learn
RLM_Learning_Action__r.Name: ProductConfig_Learn

RLM_Learning_Description__c:
Salesforce Pricing is a comprehensive, reliable, and user-friendly pricing solution. Use this comprehensive offering to manage in one centralized location, all pricing processes—from product management to calculating and applying discounts.


Salesforce Pricing Setup: Before you use Salesforce Pricing, create your basic pricing data by setting up a default pricing recipe and pricing procedure, and sync pricing data.


Pricing Operations Console: Gain valuable insights into your organisation's use of pricing data and operations.


Salesforce Pricing Access for Members: Give your partners, community, or guest users access to Salesforce Pricing so that they can manage purchases and make financial decisions.


Cost Books and Price Books: Track the expenses associated with creating or manufacturing a product in a cost book and record the product and its selling price in a price book.


Pricing Procedures: Pricing procedures are customizable, ordered stacks of pricing elements that are used to calculate the final net price of a product by using the Pricing Procedure Builder.


Salesforce Pricing creates an easy and intuitive experience through its range of predefined objects, lookup tables, and pricing recipes.


↗ Explore Pricing in Salesforce Help
Name: Price Management Get Started Popup Text Block
RLM_Learning_Action__r.Name: Price Management Get Started Popup
